summary: Sprint B — A/B/C archived-member filtering + soft delete redirect thoroughly tested. 24/24 pytest tests passed. Type C guards (POST payments/followups/annual-reviews/challenges participants) correctly return 400 when target member is archived. Type B silent filters correctly exclude archived members from GET payments, payments/late, payments/upcoming, followups, followups/upcoming, followups/missed, annual-reviews, annual-reviews/upcoming, annual-reviews/overdue, annual-reviews/stats, annual-reviews/dashboard-alerts, POST annual-reviews/auto-generate, POST payments/generate/{year}/{month}, POST notifications/send-bulk (payment_reminder and review_reminder). Warning `member_archived` correctly returned on POST payments/{id}/mark-paid and revert-to-unpaid when member archived. B.5 soft delete redirect verified on DELETE /members/{id} and DELETE /coaches/{id} with idempotency (second DELETE returns {message: 'already archived', soft_delete: true}). Regression check on POST /members + archive + restore passed.
critical_code_review_comments: core/config.get_archived_member_ids and get_archived_coach_ids load up to 5000 docs each call; for large clubs, consider caching per-request (e.g., via Request scope or fastapi Depends) to avoid repeated queries across endpoints that already do several list filters.

followups.py get_archived_member_ids() is called WITHOUT club_id on /upcoming and /missed routes (line 61, 84) while followups GET / passes club_id. Slight inconsistency — no functional bug because filter is an exclusion set, but could over-filter if a member_id collides across clubs. Recommend passing club_id for consistency.
members.py DELETE /members/{id}: good idempotent behavior. coaches.py DELETE identical pattern. Consider extracting to a shared soft_delete helper to DRY.

Sprint B covers only the owner endpoints. If other collections (e.g., challenge_checkins, activity_logs, recurring_transactions) reference member_id, verify they don't surface archived-member data in their own list endpoints before closing sprint.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed members/coaches and clean up via direct MongoDB delete_many on customer_members/coaches/payments/annual_reviews/member_followups at teardown. No production data mutated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint B fully green. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration72_sprint_b_filters.py is idempotent and self-cleaning (uses TEST_ prefix + direct pymongo teardown). Safe to re-run anytime. The challenges/auto-generate-bilans endpoint was also smoke-tested live via curl and returned 200 {created: 19}. Note: BulkNotificationRequest expects field `notification_type` (not `type`), and ChallengeParticipantCreate requires `challenge_id` in body (redundant with path param but enforced by pydantic model).

features_tested.Type C guards (400 if archived): POST /api/payments, POST /api/followups, POST /api/annual-reviews, POST /api/challenges/{id}/participants — all PASS
features_tested.Type B bulk generation filters: POST /api/payments/generate/{year}/{month}, POST /api/annual-reviews/auto-generate, POST /api/notifications/send-bulk (payment_reminder, review_reminder) — all PASS. POST /api/challenges/auto-generate-bilans smoke-tested via curl, returned 200.
features_tested.Warnings on mark-paid / revert-to-unpaid: POST /api/payments/{id}/mark-paid and /api/payments/{id}/revert-to-unpaid return warnings=['member_archived'] when target archived — PASS
features_tested.B.5 soft delete redirect: DELETE /api/members/{id} returns {message: 'Soft delete applied', soft_delete: true, archived_at: ...}, idempotent on second call returns 'already archived'. Identical behavior verified for DELETE /api/coaches/{id} — PASS
